text.title: Regression Eval Before Fix Rule Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the eval-first discipline and skip path for LLM-agent fixes.

rule.statement: For every reproducible agent incident, a failing eval case MUST be committed before the fix PR is opened. Fix PRs without a referenced eval case are blocked.
rule.rationale: Fixes without captured evals re-regress; eval-first is the only mechanism that keeps the regression suite growing.

rule.statement: The eval case MUST be deterministic (fixed seed, temperature 0, frozen tool stubs). Non-deterministic eval cases are rejected.
rule.rationale: Non-deterministic cases produce flaky CI; flaky CI is ignored.

rule.statement: Before merge, the eval case MUST flip from failing → passing AND no other eval cases may regress. CI MUST fail the PR if any eval regresses.
rule.rationale: Without a flip + no-regress check, fixes silently break other behaviours.

rule.statement: Each incident MUST produce a decision-record linking incident_id → eval_case_path → fix_pr → root-cause-class. The record is committed to the repo's `docs/regressions/` or equivalent.
rule.rationale: Audit trail per incident turns isolated fixes into an organisational learning artefact.

rule.statement: The eval-run job MUST be a required check in branch protection. Manual override is forbidden without a counter-decision-record explaining why.
rule.rationale: A required check is the only mechanism that holds the rule under PR pressure.

rule.statement: If reproduction is impossible (vendor outage, one-off transient), the incident MUST be filed as `no-fix; vendor` or `no-fix; transient` with explicit owner sign-off — never silently closed.
rule.rationale: Silent closure is how regressions return; explicit `no-fix` labels keep the suite honest.

rule.statement: If repo has no LLM agent OR incident is non-reproducible OR is a pre-prod prototype with no users, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when eval discipline cost exceeds expected regression risk.
